items.subject: Re: Centrilogic & Aptum - Customer list - Update to Technical Information
items.body_text:
[Ben Kennedy]
Hi Adam,

Thanks for that. Is it possible to also fill in the route type and next-hop (if applicable)? I prefilled in those columns assuming all subnets were directly connected. But I most likely some are not, if you could just fill in the info for those, that would be great.

Could you also confirm if you still have a presence in Telehouse North? I'm just thinking ahead if we need to interconnect our networks via a cross connect in Telehouse while customers are performing migrations.

Regards,

Ben Kennedy
Director, Network Engineering
+1 (604) 909-6406
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Subject: Re: Centrilogic & Aptum - Customer list - Update to Technical Information

Yes, please. Update the one you sent us (see below) with the migration services Aptum will be providing.

**
To support customers who require alternative colocation arrangements, Centrilogic has engaged Aptum as an approved service provider. Aptum operates data centre facilities in Heathrow and Portsmouth and is available to consult directly with affected customers to assess requirements and discuss transition options.
Available solutions include colocation services, private cloud offerings, and related infrastructure options. Aptum can also provide migration services, including planning and coordination support, to help customers transition their environments with minimal disruption. In some cases, migrating to Aptum may allow customers to retain their existing IP addresses, helping to reduce operational complexity.
Customers are under no obligation to use Aptum and remain free to pursue alternative arrangements with a provider of their choosing. This option is offered to provide flexibility and additional support during the transition process.
**

Thank you in advance. 
SERGIO BERNARDOU
Project Management Consultant

sbernardou@centrilogic.com
www.centrilogic.com

Thank you for sharing the proposed wording. Overall, this works well.
 
We would like to ask for one small adjustment to make the migration services more explicit from a customer perspective. It would be helpful to clearly state that Aptum will support the physical relocation of equipment, including on‑site assistance for disconnect, collection, packaging, transport, uninstall, and reinstall of customer racks at the Aptum facility, with customers only responsible for disconnecting and reconnecting their own cabling.
 
If Aptum is comfortable with this framing, making it explicit in the paragraph would provide additional clarity and confidence to customers during the transition.
 
Happy to discuss further if helpful.
 
 
Best regards,

To: Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>; Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com <Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com>; Adam.Nelson@aptum.com <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; george.revie@aptum.com <george.revie@aptum.com>; Peter Bell <pbell@centrilogic.com>; Joshua Justice <jjustice@centrilogic.com>; Kevin Aplin <KAplin@centrilogic.com>; Karin Vierhuis <kvierhuis@centrilogic.com>; Mark Moore <MMoore@centrilogic.com>
Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30
 

Overview of Customer Footprint
The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
Power requirements
Weight and handling constraints
Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.
 
Customer‑Specific Observations
Digital AI
Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
Cloudian
Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
Infinity
Uses one cabinet and office space.
Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
OpenText
Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
Perforce
Only cage customer at Bracknell.
Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.
 
Likelihood of Migration to Aptum
The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
De‑racking
Transportation
Re‑racking
Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.
 
Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs
Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
Connectivity details:
Port speeds (1G, 10G)
Bandwidth utilisation
Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
IP address information:
IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
Contact status:
Whether the customer has already been contacted
By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations
Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
No major technical blockers were identified:
Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
Follow‑up is required to:
Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.
 
Customer Communications
Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
It was agreed that:
Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
Aptum will contribute language describing:
Facility capabilities
Migration assistance services
The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.
 
Key Agreements
No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.
 
 
Action Items
Sergio B
Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
Aptum
Review the draft customer communication.
Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
Network & Facilities
Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
All parties
Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.
 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.

Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30
 

Overview of Customer Footprint
The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
Power requirements
Weight and handling constraints
Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.
 
Customer‑Specific Observations
Digital AI
Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
Cloudian
Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
Infinity
Uses one cabinet and office space.
Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
OpenText
Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
Perforce
Only cage customer at Bracknell.
Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.
 
Likelihood of Migration to Aptum
The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
De‑racking
Transportation
Re‑racking
Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.
 
Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs
Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
Connectivity details:
Port speeds (1G, 10G)
Bandwidth utilisation
Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
IP address information:
IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
Contact status:
Whether the customer has already been contacted
By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations
Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
No major technical blockers were identified:
Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
Follow‑up is required to:
Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.
 
Customer Communications
Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
It was agreed that:
Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
Aptum will contribute language describing:
Facility capabilities
Migration assistance services
The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.
 
Key Agreements
No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.
 
 
Action Items
Sergio B
Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
Aptum
Review the draft customer communication.
Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
Network & Facilities
Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
All parties
Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.
 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.

Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30
 

Overview of Customer Footprint
The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
Power requirements
Weight and handling constraints
Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.
 
Customer‑Specific Observations
Digital AI
Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
Cloudian
Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
Infinity
Uses one cabinet and office space.
Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
OpenText
Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
Perforce
Only cage customer at Bracknell.
Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.
 
Likelihood of Migration to Aptum
The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
De‑racking
Transportation
Re‑racking
Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.
 
Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs
Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
Connectivity details:
Port speeds (1G, 10G)
Bandwidth utilisation
Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
IP address information:
IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
Contact status:
Whether the customer has already been contacted
By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations
Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
No major technical blockers were identified:
Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
Follow‑up is required to:
Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.
 
Customer Communications
Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
It was agreed that:
Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
Aptum will contribute language describing:
Facility capabilities
Migration assistance services
The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.
 
Key Agreements
No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.
 
 
Action Items
Sergio B
Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
Aptum
Review the draft customer communication.
Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
Network & Facilities
Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
All parties
Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.
 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.
SERGIO BERNARDOU

Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30
 

Overview of Customer Footprint
The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
Power requirements
Weight and handling constraints
Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.
 
Customer‑Specific Observations
Digital AI
Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
Cloudian
Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
Infinity
Uses one cabinet and office space.
Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
OpenText
Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
Perforce
Only cage customer at Bracknell.
Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.
 
Likelihood of Migration to Aptum
The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
De‑racking
Transportation
Re‑racking
Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.
 
Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs
Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
Connectivity details:
Port speeds (1G, 10G)
Bandwidth utilisation
Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
IP address information:
IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
Contact status:
Whether the customer has already been contacted
By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations
Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
No major technical blockers were identified:
Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
Follow‑up is required to:
Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.
 
Customer Communications
Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
It was agreed that:
Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
Aptum will contribute language describing:
Facility capabilities
Migration assistance services
The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.
 
Key Agreements
No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.
 
 
Action Items
Sergio B
Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
Aptum
Review the draft customer communication.
Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
Network & Facilities
Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
All parties
Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.
 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.

**
To support customers who require alternative colocation arrangements, Centrilogic has engaged Aptum as an approved service provider. Aptum operates data centre facilities in Heathrow and Portsmouth and is available to consult directly with affected customers to assess requirements and discuss transition options.
Available solutions include colocation services, private cloud offerings, and related infrastructure options. Aptum can also provide migration services, including planning and coordination support, to help customers transition their environments with minimal disruption. In some cases, migrating to Aptum may allow customers to retain their existing IP addresses, helping to reduce operational complexity.
Customers are under no obligation to use Aptum and remain free to pursue alternative arrangements with a provider of their choosing. This option is offered to provide flexibility and additional support during the transition process.

Sergio,
 
Below is what I suggest for the third paragraph:
 
**
To support customers who require alternative colocation arrangements, Centrilogic has engaged Aptum as an approved service provider. Aptum operates data centre facilities in Heathrow and Portsmouth and is available to consult directly with affected customers to assess requirements and discuss transition options.
Available solutions include colocation services, private cloud offerings, and related infrastructure options. Aptum can also provide migration services, including planning and coordination support, to help customers transition their environments with minimal disruption. In some cases, migrating to Aptum may allow customers to retain their existing IP addresses, helping to reduce operational complexity.
Customers are under no obligation to use Aptum and remain free to pursue alternative arrangements with a provider of their choosing. This option is offered to provide flexibility and additional support during the transition process.
**

From: Sergio Bernardou <sbernardou@centrilogic.com>
Sent: Monday, March 30, 2026 11:51 AM
To: Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>; Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com <Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com>; Adam.Nelson@aptum.com <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; george.revie@aptum.com <george.revie@aptum.com>; Peter Bell <pbell@centrilogic.com>; Joshua Justice <jjustice@centrilogic.com>; Kevin Aplin <KAplin@centrilogic.com>; Karin Vierhuis <kvierhuis@centrilogic.com>; Mark Moore <MMoore@centrilogic.com>
Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30
 

Overview of Customer Footprint
The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
Power requirements
Weight and handling constraints
Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.
 
Customer‑Specific Observations
Digital AI
Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
Cloudian
Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
Infinity
Uses one cabinet and office space.
Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
OpenText
Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
Perforce
Only cage customer at Bracknell.
Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.
 
Likelihood of Migration to Aptum
The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
De‑racking
Transportation
Re‑racking
Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.
 
Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs
Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
Connectivity details:
Port speeds (1G, 10G)
Bandwidth utilisation
Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
IP address information:
IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
Contact status:
Whether the customer has already been contacted
By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations
Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
No major technical blockers were identified:
Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
Follow‑up is required to:
Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.
 
Customer Communications
Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
It was agreed that:
Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
Aptum will contribute language describing:
Facility capabilities
Migration assistance services
The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.
 
Key Agreements
No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.
 
 
Action Items
Sergio B
Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
Aptum
Review the draft customer communication.
Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
Network & Facilities
Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
All parties
Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.
 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.
SERGIO BERNARDOU
Project Management Consultant
 
sbernardou@centrilogic.com
www.centrilogic.com
Follow us: Linkedin | X

items.subject: [Sommet sur la cybersécurité et l’IA 2026] La nouvelle réalité de la sécurité IA
items.body_text:
[Fortinet]
View email online​

AMERICAS

21 avril
9 h 00 (PDT) // 12 h 00 (EDT)

Sommet sur la cybersécurité et l’IA 2026 
Sécuriser l’IA sans compromettre l’avenir 
Inscrivez-vous maintenant

Bonjour Adam,
​
Les décisions en matière de sécurité ne se prennent rarement en une seule fois. Elles s’accumulent, et les choix initiaux peuvent limiter silencieusement ce qui sera possible par la suite.
​
Le Sommet sur la cybersécurité et l’IA 2026 explore comment les organisations abordent la sécurité alors que les nouvelles capacités évoluent plus rapidement que les modèles existants n’ont été conçus pour le supporter, avec la perspective de Neil MacDonald, Distinguished VP Analyst chez Gartner.
​
Lors du sommet, nous explorerons :

Quelles décisions en matière de sécurité de l’IA ne peuvent être prises qu’une seule fois

Où la visibilité et la gouvernance commencent à se fragiliser

Comment l’IA concentre les risques dans des zones inattendues

Comment avancer sans créer une exposition à long terme

Si vous évaluez des décisions qui auront un impact au-delà du prochain cycle de planification, ce sommet vous apportera une vision claire de la façon dont d’autres organisations équilibrent progrès, flexibilité et gestion des risques.
Inscrivez-vous maintenant

Sous-titrage disponible pendant l’événement.

Cordialement,
Équipe Fortinet

Fortify Your Networks

Manage Your Subscription | Privacy Policy | Contact us | Chat with Us

  

 

 

 

 

 

 

Fortinet, Inc. | 909 Kifer Road | Sunnyvale, CA 94086 | +1-408-235-7700
© 2026 Copyright | This email was sent to adam.nelson@aptum.com.

Hello Matthew, thank you for speaking with me.  
 
We are excited about the prospects of embarking on a Net Terms relationship with Aptum. 
 
Per our conversation, please send the interim 2026 six month financials including the balance sheet, income statement, and if available, the statement of cash flows and we will make you folks our top priority for a review assessment.  
 
From what I have experienced, the PMSI typically comes into play when 1): you are changing banks.  Banks like to be in first place.  As long as your account is not very far past due with us and as long as the new bank is financing your growth(ie: Arbitrarily speaking, if former bank was extending Aptum a $10M line and the new bank is only extending a $25K line and TD Synnex has a $1M line, we would want to discuss) , we would subordinate our position behind the new bank.  Or 2): if you firm unexpectedly files for bankruptcy protection or 3): your firm is purchased by a Private Equity firm, etc. they may ask us to subordinate.  
 
Benefits of the PMSI
 
1): we can set a higher net terms line than we can without it
 
2): after some history of on time payments, the PMSI opens up our RSA and other programs.  RSA for example would allow us to accommodate large sales orders to strong end-users outside of your net terms line so that the large order does not tie up your internal line,  Fee is 1% of the purchase order for the first $120K with a maximum fee of $1,200 so the program works well with very large million dollar orders as there is no additional fee after $1,200.  Program entails your end user customer paying a bank lockbox that TD Synnex controls.  We can remain 100% invisible to your end user.  We receive payment at the bank lockbox we control and we then turnaround 24 hours and send your firm an eft/ach payment. 
 
3): with some history of on time payments and a signed Amplify Addendum, the PMSI opens up our Amplify extended terms program for Net 45, Net 60 etc.  
 

Patrick Michael Molen, CCE, CICP
Manager I, Credit and Collections SMB, MSP, Retail, Sony

items.body_text:
[Connect CRE Digital Infrastructure & AI]
Amazon goes direct-to-device ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­   

 

 

 

 

April 15, 2026

 Enacting a Moratorium

Even as billions of dollars have been committed to development, the potential environmental and utility infrastructure impacts of large-scale data centers have given rise to public opposition. A bill enacted Tuesday by the Maine Legislature may impose the first statewide moratorium on new construction of larger facilities.
 
With some tech firms already laying off employees to make way for expanded artificial intelligence use, concerns have also surfaced about the impact of AI on employment. A new report from Newmark aims to separate fact from fiction when it comes to defining the future of office space in the coming era of AI.

 

TOP STORY

 

Maine May Become First State to Enact Moratorium on New Data Centers
The bill, which awaits the governor’s signature, would freeze approvals on construction of facilities requiring more than 20 megawatts of power until fall 2027

CONNECT CRE

Understanding the AI-Office Space Connection

A Newmark report explains likely employment scenarios as more companies utilize AI and their impact on office space

CONNECT CRE

Lincoln Property Company JV to Develop Data Centers in Kansas City
Metrobloks is partnering with Lincoln on the project, to be built on land already zoned and powered for data center use

CONNECT MONEY

Amazon to Acquire Globalstar to Expand D2D Satellite Connectivity
The acquisition will enable Amazon’s Leo satellite network to deliver direct-to-device connectivity and expand coverage to areas beyond traditional wireless infrastructure

CONNECT CANADA CRE

Invest Ontario Welcomes Microsoft’s Data Centre Expansion Plans

Microsoft’s expansion of its Azure Canada Central region is expected to support 1,250 jobs and strengthen the province’s growing cloud and AI infrastructure

Visit ConnectCRE.com 

 

Share Your News
Have a press release, announcement, or research to share?

Send to: pbubny@connectcre.com 

 

 
 
 
 

Never Miss An Update. Add nationalnewsroom@connect.media to your trusted contacts.  Using Gmail? Drag this email to your "Primary" tab for seamless access. Need help? Click here for tips on ensuring delivery to your inbox.

Connect Group Media, 3900 San Fernando Rd, Suite 1002, Glendale, California 91204, US

items.subject: Helping Aptum Win More Govtech Deals — Let's Talk
items.body_text:
[Peter Famiglietti]
You don't often get email from famiglietti@insightaottechnologies.com. Learn why this is important 

 Hi ,

I'm Peter Famiglietti, Strategic Partnerships at AOT Technologies. We've spent a decade building and delivering technology solutions for government agencies, educational institutions, and public-sector organizations across North America.

I came across Aptum  work in the IT - Govtech space and wanted to connect.

Why we might be relevant to you:

→  SLED360: Our platform is built from the ground up for state, local, education, and government programs
→  Workflow automation: Powering live programs across BC Government, healthcare, and enterprise
→  Cloud engineering, DevSecOps, and AI: Active delivery, not just advisory

For select alliance partners, we open up the full stack — so you can expand what you offer your SLED clients without adding internal overhead.

Would a 30-minute discovery call make sense this week?

Best regards,
Peter Famiglietti, MBA, PEng
Principal - Alliances, Partnerships, Co-Marketing 
Canada | USA | India
LinkedIn

items.subject: Re: DBA Support Contract Renewal Discussion- Aptum Technologies || Accenture(Navisite)
items.body_text:
[Jason Auer]
Hey Vinamra, can you confirm the last day of support services and confirm the contract end date?

Jason Auer
Manager, Customer Support
+1 (678) 365-2800
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Setia, Arjun <arjun.setia@accenture.com>
Sent: Monday, March 23, 2026 11:06 AM
To: Jason Auer <Jason.Auer@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <adam.nelson@aptum.com>; Ramawat, Vinamra <vinamra.ramawat@accenture.com>
Cc: Talwar, Vikas <vikas.s.talwar@accenture.com>; Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: DBA Support Contract Renewal Discussion- Aptum Technologies || Accenture(Navisite) 
 

Hi Jason,
 
Thank you for letting us know, and I must admit it's a bit sad to hear about your decision not to renew the support contract. With Accenture, our services and portfolio have expanded significantly, so please do keep us in mind if anything comes up in the future and you'd like to partner again. I wish you all the best as you transition to bringing this in-house.
 
I'm looping in Vinamra, our Client Success Manager, to take things forward. Vinamra will review the contract end date and confirm the last day our support services will be available to you. Rest assured, support will continue as usual until the contract officially concludes. As per the legacy contract, I believe there's a 90-day notice period, but Vinamra will double-check and get back to you with the exact details.
 
Thank you again for your partnership over the years, and please don’t hesitate to reach out if you need anything down the road.
 
Best regards,
 
 

 
Arjun Setia – Navisite, Part of Accenture 

Pronouns: (He/His) 
Account Manager
Gurugram, India
Mobile: +91 9467630402
accenture.com
Navisite.com 

 
 
 

From: Jason Auer <Jason.Auer@aptum.com> 
Sent: Monday, March 23, 2026 10:59 AM
To: Setia, Arjun <arjun.setia@accenture.com>; Adam Nelson <adam.nelson@aptum.com>
Cc: Talwar, Vikas <vikas.s.talwar@accenture.com>; Ramawat, Vinamra <vinamra.ramawat@accenture.com>; Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>
Subject: [External] Re: DBA Support Contract Renewal Discussion- Aptum Technologies || Accenture(Navisite)
 

WARNING: External email. Be vigilant with links, attachments, and requests.
 

Good morning Arjun, I wanted to reach out to let you know that after some internal discussions we have made the decision to bring this in house and will not be renewing the support contract in April.  Big thanks to you guys and the support you have provided over the years. 

 

Jason Auer

Manager, Customer Support
+1 (678) 365-2800
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Setia, Arjun <arjun.setia@accenture.com>
Sent: Friday, March 6, 2026 5:15 PM
To: Adam Nelson <adam.nelson@aptum.com>; Jason Auer <jason.auer@aptum.com>
Cc: Talwar, Vikas <vikas.s.talwar@accenture.com>; Ramawat, Vinamra <vinamra.ramawat@accenture.com>
Subject: DBA Support Contract Renewal Discussion- Aptum Technologies || Accenture(Navisite)

 

You don't often get email from arjun.setia@accenture.com. Learn why this is important
Hi Adam and Jason,
 
It is great to reconnect with you both! I hope you are doing well. I was reaching to Mark Bolen for this but was made aware he had left Aptum recently.
 
I wanted to touch base regarding your upcoming renewal on DBA support services with Navisite/Accenture. We believe this is the perfect time to review and ensure that all terms and conditions align with the evolving needs of your business.
 
As you may be aware, we were acquired by Accenture a while back. We believe that renewals present an excellent opportunity to connect and make sure we are up to date with the latest business requirements. I will shortly share the new renewal contract for your review and have attached previous signed amendment for quick review; however, I wanted to ensure if there is something that you would like to incorporate/make changes etc. so let me know so that I can make those changes accordingly.
 
Currently, you have bucket of 25 hours spread across various DB platforms like Oracle, MS SQL and MySQL(attached the previous amendment for quick reference) Would you like to make any changes to this? Also, what sort of hours bifurcation you would like to have between this various DB platforms as we need to specify going forward?
 
I appreciate your feedback and response and accordingly I will share the new renewal contract for your review.
 
 

 
Arjun Setia – Navisite, Part of Accenture 

Pronouns: (He/His) 
Account Manager
Gurugram, India
Mobile: +91 9467630402
accenture.com
Navisite.com 

 
 

 

This message is for the designated recipient only and may contain privileged, proprietary, or otherwise confidential information. If you have received it in error, please notify the sender immediately and delete the original. Any other use of the e-mail by you is prohibited. Where allowed by local law, electronic communications with Accenture and its affiliates, including e-mail and instant messaging (including content), may be scanned by our systems for the purposes of information security, AI-powered support capabilities, and assessment of internal compliance with Accenture policy. Your privacy is important to us. Accenture uses your personal data only in compliance with data protection laws. For further information on how Accenture processes your personal data, please see our privacy statement at https://www.accenture.com/us-en/privacy-policy.
______________________________________________________________________________________

www.accenture.com

This email is sent for and on behalf of Aptum Technologies (USA) Inc with US head office located at 2300 NW 89th Place Miami, FL 33172. This email and any attachments are confidential and intended for the recipient only. If you have received this message in error, please notify the sender immediately, destroy this email and any attachments, and do not use, copy, store and/or disclose to any person this email and any attachments.

If you no longer wish to receive promotional emails from Aptum Technologies our privacy policy outlines how you can request that your details be removed from our mailing systems.

items.subject: Does Aptum handle IT?
items.body_text:
[Sally Cabrera]
[You don't often get email from sally@amplixityomegastack.shop. Learn why this is important at https://aka.ms/LearnAboutSenderIdentification ]

I wanted to check in regarding my earlier message Adam. I understand that messages can get buried in the inbox.

I recommend watching our interview with Steve Taplin, CEO of Sonatafy, where he shares how he consistently books 2-8 meetings each day and has built such a powerful marketing system that he often has to decline new prospects.

https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fyoutu.be%2FEJCHAbRNluI&data=05%7C02%7Cadam.nelson%40aptum.com%7C3c7deabb638f432b4e5408de9b18bd57%7Cad69d4224fa742b0a4d3f58410f311ed%7C1%7C0%7C639118727735034760%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C4000%7C%7C%7C&sdata=h8S6mw%2FJMJl7yMSh66sBupQtpH9pbAStS288gbfNkFY%3D&reserved=0 [https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fyoutu.be%2FEJCHAbRNluI&data=05%7C02%7Cadam.nelson%40aptum.com%7C3c7deabb638f432b4e5408de9b18bd57%7Cad69d4224fa742b0a4d3f58410f311ed%7C1%7C0%7C639118727735060602%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C4000%7C%7C%7C&sdata=0js9OMaJ9Jk7wjfu8ayP5vIFtK5dsTsP2jmX%2BYjW1AE%3D&reserved=0]
https://can01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.youtube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv%3DEJCHAbRNluI&data=05%7C02%7Cadam.nelson%40aptum.com%7C3c7deabb638f432b4e5408de9b18bd57%7Cad69d4224fa742b0a4d3f58410f311ed%7C1%7C0%7C639118727735077752%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C4000%7C%7C%7C&sdata=W%2FucPMRsAhI3c3IKS3ot%2BKflpuRIL0I1a4vtCazn6o8%3D&reserved=0
If you’d like to learn about the exact system we created for them and are interested in getting a similar one with a 100% guarantee, would you be available for a brief 15-minute call sometime this week?


On Tue, Apr 14, 2026 at 5:03 PM Sally Cabrera <sally@amplixityomegastack.shop> wrote:



> Hi Adam,
>
> As Vice President Operations at Aptum, you're likely always looking for a more predictable way to generate revenue.
>
>
> We recently built a revenue generation system for CloudFit Software that produced $1.5$1M in new revenue achieved within only 4 months The approach: identify their best-fit accounts, pre-warm them with targeted ads for pennies on the dollar, and run multi-touch outreach to drive real conversations.
>
> Across B2B IT service companies, we've observed comparable outcomes — from securing 7-figure contracts like Informa Software, to consistently scheduling 2–8 meetings daily and doubling Sonatafy Technology's revenue.
>
> If we could build the same revenue generation system for Aptum (backed by a 100% guarantee), would a 15-minute call this week make sense to explore fit?
>
>
> Sally Cabrera | Strategic Director

items.subject: Galion Acquires Five RONA-Branded Stores on Montreal’s South Shore for $100M-Plus
items.body_text:
[Connect CRE News: Canada]
You don't often get email from newsroomcanada@connect.media. Learn why this is important 

 

 

 
 

Galion Acquires Five RONA-Branded Stores on Montreal’s South Shore for $100M-Plus 

 

Galion has completed the largest transaction in its history with the acquisition of five large-format RONA and RONA L’Entrepot stores on Montreal’s South Shore for more than $100 million.
 
The properties are located in Saint-Bruno-de-Montarville, Que.; Brossard, Que.; Granby, Que.; Beloeil, Que; and Saint-Hyacinthe, Que., adding approximately 620,000 square feet to the company’s portfolio.
 
Read more ...

Get Connect Daily 

Submit a Press Release 

Connect Group Media, 3900 San Fernando Rd, Glendale, California 91204 US.
Manage subscription to Connect CRE News: Canada.

items.subject: Expedient Service Interruption Notice - Informational Update (Resolved) Ticket #3679897
items.body_text:
[no-reply@expedient.com]
Adam Nelson, 
The service interruption that we notified you about regarding the Elastic Platform is RESOLVED. We appreciate your patience. The master case number for this incident is 3679897. We will send follow-up information via e-mail within the next two business days.
__________________________________________
Sent: Monday, April 13 2026 1:30 PM EDT
Subject: Expedient RE: Service Interruption Notice - Informational Update (Ongoing) Ticket #3679897

The service interruption that we notified you about regarding the Elastic platform is still ongoing. Performance continues to remain stable, with the main impact being a lack of redundancy. Expedient engineers and our vendors are working to balance the load across the Elastic platform. If you are experiencing instability or degraded performance, please contact the Operations Support Center (OSC).

We apologize for the inconvenience. The case number for this incident is 3679897. We will send another update by 1:30 PM ET, 4/15.
__________________________________________
Sent: Friday, April 10 2026 11:29 AM EDT
Subject: Expedient RE: Service Interruption Notice - Informational Update (Ongoing) Ticket #3679897

The service interruption that we notified you about regarding the Elastic platform is still ongoing. Performance is currently stable, with the main impact being a lack of redundancy. Our engineers are continuing to troubleshoot in collaboration with multiple vendors. Additionally, we are still working on adding additional resources to reach a full resolution as quickly as possible. If you are experiencing instability or degraded performance, please contact the Operations Support Center (OSC).

We apologize for the inconvenience. The case number for this incident is 3679897. We will send another update by 1:30 PM ET, 4/13.
__________________________________________
Sent: Thursday, April 9 2026 11:54 AM EDT
Subject: Expedient RE: Service Interruption Notice - Informational Update (Ongoing) Ticket #3679897

The service interruption that we notified you about regarding the Elastic platform is still ongoing. Performance is currently stable with the main impact being a lack of redundancy. Our engineers are continuing to troubleshoot in collaboration with multiple vendors. Additionally, we are still working on adding additional resources to reach a full resolution as quickly as possible. If you are experiencing instability or degraded performance, please contact the Operations Support Center (OSC).

We apologize for the inconvenience. The case number for this incident is 3679897. We will send another update by 11:30 AM ET tomorrow, 4/10.
__________________________________________
Sent: Wednesday, April 8 2026 11:30 AM EDT
Subject: Expedient RE: Service Interruption Notice - Informational Update (Ongoing)

The service interruption that we notified you about regarding the Elastic platform is still ongoing. Performance is currently stable with the main impact being a lack of redundancy. Our engineers are continuing to troubleshoot, work with the vendor, and add additional resources to reach a full resolution as quickly as possible. If you are experiencing instability or degraded performance, please contact the Operations Support Center (OSC).

We apologize for the inconvenience. The case number for this incident is 3679897. We will send another update by 11:30 AM ET tomorrow, 4/9.

__________________________________________
Sent: Tuesday, April 7 2026 11:30 AM EDT
Subject: Expedient RE: Service Interruption Notice - Informational Update (Ongoing) Ticket #3679897

The service interruption that we notified you about regarding the Elastic platform is still ongoing, with the main impact being a lack of redundancy. Our engineers are continuing to troubleshoot and additionally escalating our case with the vendor to reach a resolution as quickly as possible. If you are experiencing instability or degraded performance, please contact the Operations Support Center (OSC).

We apologize for the inconvenience. The case number for this incident is 3679897. We will send another update by 11:30 AM ET tomorrow, 4/8.

__________________________________________
Sent: Monday, April 6 2026 11:30 AM EDT
Subject: Expedient RE: Service Interruption Notice - Informational Update (Ongoing) Ticket #3679897

The service interruption that we notified you about regarding the Elastic platform is still ongoing.

Currently, the impact is primarily a lack of redundancy across the platform. Our engineers are continuing to work on troubleshooting efforts to implement a full resolution. If you are experiencing instability or degraded performance, please contact the Operations Support Center (OSC).

We apologize for the inconvenience. The case number for this incident is 3679897. We will send another update by 11:30 AM ET tomorrow, 4/7.
__________________________________________
Sent: Sunday, April 5 2026 5:30 PM EDT
Subject: Expedient RE: Service Interruption Notice - Informational Update (Ongoing) Ticket #3679897

The service interruption that we notified you about regarding the Elastic platform is still ongoing.

Our engineers have implemented additional adjustments and are still working; some changes will take several hours and may run overnight. If you are experiencing instability or degraded performance, please contact the Operations Support Center (OSC).

We apologize for the inconvenience. The case number for this incident is 3679897. We will send another update by 11:30 AM ET tomorrow, 4/6.
__________________________________________
Sent: Sunday, April 5 2026 11:30 AM EDT
Subject: Expedient RE: Service Interruption Notice - Informational Update (Ongoing) Ticket #3679897

The service interruption that we notified you about regarding the Elastic platform is still ongoing.

Performance remains degraded but stable and our troubleshooting efforts are ongoing to reach a full resolution. If you are still experiencing instability or degraded performance, please contact the Operations Support Center (OSC).

We apologize for the inconvenience. The case number for this incident is 3679897. We will send another update by 5:30 PM ET.
__________________________________________
Sent: Sunday, April 5 2026 1:17 AM EDT
Subject: Expedient RE: RE: RE: RE: Service Interruption Notice - Informational Update (Ongoing) - Pittsburgh - Allegheny Center Mall Ticket #3679897

The service interruption that we notified you about regarding the Elastic platform is still ongoing.

Expedient engineers continue to perform maintenance on the platform, and work remains underway to fully restore optimal performance. While performance has not been fully restored, we believe the platform is stable at this time. If you are still experiencing instability or degraded performance, please contact the Operations Support Center (OSC).

We apologize for the inconvenience. The case number for this incident is 3679897. We will send another update by 11:30 AM ET.

__________________________________________
Sent: Sunday, April 5 2026 12:04 AM EDT
Subject: Expedient RE: RE: RE: Service Interruption Notice - Informational Update (Ongoing) - Pittsburgh - Allegheny Center Mall Ticket #3679897

The service interruption that we notified you about regarding the Elastic platform is still ongoing.

Expedient engineers continue to perform maintenance on the platform, and work remains underway to fully restore optimal performance. Expedient Engineers rebooted an allocator node supporting the Elastic platform, and are now checking client deployments and restarting any that are still showing as unhealthy. If you are still experiencing instability or degraded performance, please contact the Operations Support Center (OSC).

We apologize for the inconvenience. The case number for this incident is 3679897. We will send another update by 3:00 AM ET.

__________________________________________
Sent: Saturday, April 4 2026 7:50 PM EDT
Subject: Expedient RE: RE: Service Interruption Notice - Informational Update (Ongoing) - Pittsburgh - Allegheny Center Mall Ticket #3679897

The service interruption that we notified you about regarding the Elastic platform is still ongoing.

Expedient engineers continue to perform maintenance on the platform, and work remains underway to fully restore optimal performance. The internal migration we mentioned in our last update is still ongoing. Once that is complete, we plan to restart an unhealthy service as a next step. If you are still experiencing instability or degraded performance, please contact the Operations Support Center (OSC).

We apologize for the inconvenience. The case number for this incident is 3679897. We will send another update by 11:00 PM ET.

__________________________________________
Sent: Saturday, April 4 2026 6:30 PM EDT
Subject: Expedient RE: Service Interruption Notice - Informational Update (Ongoing) - Pittsburgh - Allegheny Center Mall Ticket #3679897

The service interruption that we notified you about regarding the Elastic platform is still ongoing.

Expedient engineers continue to perform maintenance on the platform, and work remains underway to fully restore optimal performance. An internal Expedient deployment on the affected allocator is being migrated to alleviate load and disk pressure. If you are still experiencing instability or degraded performance, please contact the Operations Support Center (OSC).

We apologize for the inconvenience. The case number for this incident is 3679897. We will send another update by 8:30 PM ET.

__________________________________________
Sent: Saturday, April 4 2026 5:30 PM EDT
Subject: Expedient RE: Service Interruption Notice - Informational Update (Ongoing) - Pittsburgh - Allegheny Center Mall Ticket #3679897

The service interruption that we notified you about regarding the Elastic platform is still ongoing. Expedient engineers continue to perform maintenance on the platform, and work remains underway to fully restore optimal performance.

You may notice slowness when accessing data for instances whose storage is on the affected allocator; this can result in slower searches, delayed ingests, or longer dashboard load times. If you are still experiencing instability or degraded performance, please contact the Operations Support Center (OSC).

We apologize for the inconvenience. The case number for this incident is 3679897. We will send another update by 6:30 PM ET.
__________________________________________
Sent: Saturday, April 4 2026 3:14 PM EDT
Subject: Expedient Service Interruption Notice - Informational Update (Ongoing) - Pittsburgh - Allegheny Center Mall Ticket #3679897

The service interruption that we notified you about regarding the Elastic platform is ongoing.

During this interruption, you may have experienced slow query responses, delayed log ingestion, dashboard timeouts, or intermittent connectivity issues when accessing your Elastic services. Some monitoring alerts may have been delayed or missing, and data visualization refreshes could have taken significantly longer.

Expedient engineers are actively performing maintenance, which has already started to reduce performance degradation. Work continues to fully restore optimal performance. If you're still experiencing instability, please contact the Operation Support Center (OSC).

We apologize for the inconvenience. The case number for this incident is 3679897. We will send another update by 5:30 PM ET.

__________________________________________
Sent: Saturday, April 4 2026 2:07 PM EDT
Subject: Expedient Service Interruption Notice - Initial - Pittsburgh - Allegheny Center Mall Ticket #3679897

We have identified a service interruption on our Elastic platform that may be affecting your services and causing performance degradation. Our engineering team is actively investigating the root cause and assessing the impact. This notification serves to uphold our commitment to inform you of events that may affect your services proactively. We will provide an update within the next 60 minutes with further details. 
Sincerely, 

The Expedient Operations Support Center 
You are receiving this email because you are a client contact for Aptum. If you don't want to receive this type of email in the future, please configure your email and recorded telephone message notification settings at https://support.expedient.com/settings. If you are having trouble seeing this email, you can view this notification online at https://support.expedient.com/notification/overview/id/62381. 
© 2026 Expedient

items.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL]RE: Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change
items.body_text:
[Marshbanks, Rick]
Hi Matt/Team – I greatly welcome the opportunity to continue to support Aptum’s AWS partnership for the US. I also can confirm no action is required on your side as the new SPP discounts have already gone into effect. See attached profitability framework. 
 
Let me know your thoughts on a cadence frequency moving forward. There is a lot we can accomplish if we can put our two thinking heads together! 😊 

Respectfully, 
 

Rick Marshbanks 
AWS Business Development Executive
St Petersburg, FL 
(248) 622.0869
 
 

From: Matthew Carter Matthew.Carter@aptum.com 
Sent: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 11:59 AM
To: Marshbanks, Rick Rick.Marshbanks@ingrammicro.com; Erik Tanguay Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com
Cc: Horwath, John John.Horwath@ingrammicro.com; Olvera, Israel Israel.Olvera2@ingrammicro.com; Rosario, Jonathan Jonathan.Rosario@ingrammicro.com; Jennings, Adam Adam.Jennings@ingrammicro.com; Jaroenchonwanit, Lalita Lalita.Jaroenchonwanit@ingrammicro.com; Chandrashekar, Subramanya Subramanya.Chandrashekar@ingrammicro.com; Adam Nelson Adam.Nelson@aptum.com; Andrei Ianouchkevitch Andrei.Ianouchkevitch@aptum.com; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: [EXTERNAL]RE: Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change
 
Hi Rick,
We’re encountering some challenges with onboarding to your competitor, and there’s a possibility that our U.S. business will remain with Ingram for the near term.
 
If we proceed in that direction, please let me know if there are any steps or requirements on our end to ensure continuity and ensure the flow-through of the 8%.
 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain
+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Sent: March 20, 2026 2:11 PM
To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com>
Cc: Horwath, John <John.Horwath@ingrammicro.com>; Olvera, Israel <Israel.Olvera2@ingrammicro.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Rosario, Jonathan <Jonathan.Rosario@ingrammicro.com>; Jennings, Adam <Adam.Jennings@ingrammicro.com>; Jaroenchonwanit, Lalita <Lalita.Jaroenchonwanit@ingrammicro.com>; Chandrashekar, Subramanya <Subramanya.Chandrashekar@ingrammicro.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Andrei Ianouchkevitch <Andrei.Ianouchkevitch@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: [EXTERNAL]RE: Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change
 
Hi Matt – I can only speak on the US discounts – 8% will be the new base margin, starting April 1st. 
 
Respectfully, 
 

Rick Marshbanks 
AWS Business Development Executive
St Petersburg, FL 
(248) 622.0869
 
 

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com> 
Sent: Friday, March 20, 2026 2:09 PM
To: Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com>; Marshbanks, Rick <Rick.Marshbanks@ingrammicro.com>
Cc: Horwath, John <John.Horwath@ingrammicro.com>; Olvera, Israel <Israel.Olvera2@ingrammicro.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Rosario, Jonathan <Jonathan.Rosario@ingrammicro.com>; Jennings, Adam <Adam.Jennings@ingrammicro.com>; Jaroenchonwanit, Lalita <Lalita.Jaroenchonwanit@ingrammicro.com>; Chandrashekar, Subramanya <Subramanya.Chandrashekar@ingrammicro.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Andrei Ianouchkevitch <Andrei.Ianouchkevitch@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>
Subject: [EXTERNAL]RE: Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change
 
Hi Rick,
Wanted to underscore the highlight below.
 
Please confirm,
 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain
+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com> 
Sent: March 19, 2026 4:41 PM
To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Marshbanks, Rick <Rick.Marshbanks@ingrammicro.com>
Cc: Horwath, John <John.Horwath@ingrammicro.com>; Olvera, Israel <Israel.Olvera2@ingrammicro.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Rosario, Jonathan <Jonathan.Rosario@ingrammicro.com>; Jennings, Adam <Adam.Jennings@ingrammicro.com>; Jaroenchonwanit, Lalita <Lalita.Jaroenchonwanit@ingrammicro.com>; Chandrashekar, Subramanya <Subramanya.Chandrashekar@ingrammicro.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Andrei Ianouchkevitch <Andrei.Ianouchkevitch@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change
 

Hi Rick,

 

It has been awhile.  I just wanted to advise you that I put in the AWS Outbound Transfer Request for the selected accounts that we are transitioning.  Please advise if there is anything missing with the provided information on the form.

 

The other note, which Matt asked earlier, is the confirmation of the 8% <discount> on the accounts that will remain with Ingram Micro.  I know you only represent the US Geography, I do see Lalita on the email thread, maybe she can oversee that gets passed along as of April 1, 2026.

 

Thank you

 

Erik

 

Erik Tanguay

Project Control Officer
+1 (514) 673-7552
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, March 17, 2026 9:51 AM
To: Marshbanks, Rick <Rick.Marshbanks@ingrammicro.com>
Cc: Horwath, John <John.Horwath@ingrammicro.com>; Olvera, Israel <Israel.Olvera2@ingrammicro.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Rosario, Jonathan <Jonathan.Rosario@ingrammicro.com>; Jennings, Adam <Adam.Jennings@ingrammicro.com>; Jaroenchonwanit, Lalita <Lalita.Jaroenchonwanit@ingrammicro.com>; Chandrashekar, Subramanya <Subramanya.Chandrashekar@ingrammicro.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Andrei Ianouchkevitch <Andrei.Ianouchkevitch@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change 

 

Hi Rick,
Thanks for your collaboration here, again, we’re only porting US customers, but will remain with Ingram for the UK and Canadian regions.
 
On that note, can you confirm steps to ensure we’ll receive the 8% discount in those geographies effective 4/1?
 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain
+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Marshbanks, Rick <Rick.Marshbanks@ingrammicro.com> 
Sent: February 18, 2026 5:33 PM
To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Cc: Horwath, John <John.Horwath@ingrammicro.com>; Olvera, Israel <Israel.Olvera2@ingrammicro.com>; Marc Pare <marc.pare@aptum.com>; Rosario, Jonathan <Jonathan.Rosario@ingrammicro.com>; Jennings, Adam <Adam.Jennings@ingrammicro.com>; Jaroenchonwanit, Lalita <Lalita.Jaroenchonwanit@ingrammicro.com>; Chandrashekar, Subramanya <Subramanya.Chandrashekar@ingrammicro.com>; Adam Nelson <adam.nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change
 
+@Chandrashekar, Subramanya / @Jaroenchonwanit, Lalita
 
Hi Matt, Thank you for the note and for the partnership we have built together. While I am disappointed to hear we will not be with you for the next phase of your AWS Partner Journey, I sincerely appreciate the collaboration and trust Aptum has placed in Ingram Micro throughout our relationship. Given the April 1 transition timeline, I welcome the opportunity to schedule a brief call with the broader team to review the offboarding process to help ensure everything runs smoothly. This would also allow us to align on any country-specific considerations and required documentation so there are no disruptions.
 
I would also value the opportunity to better understand the factors that led up to this decision. If there are areas where we could have delivered more value, I want to ensure we take that feedback constructively as we continue to improve and adapt as a global business unit.  If you prefer, I am happy to connect one-on-one as well. Please let me know what works best for you and your team.
 
Regardless of the path forward, we remain committed to supporting Aptum through a seamless transition and keeping the door open for future collaboration. 
 
Respectfully, RM 
 

Rick Marshbanks 
AWS Business Development Executive
St Petersburg, FL 
(248) 622.0869
 

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 4:03 PM
To: AWS Cloud Operations <awscloudops@ingrammicro.com>; Rosario, Jonathan <Jonathan.Rosario@ingrammicro.com>
Cc: Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: [EXTERNAL]Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change 

 

Dear Ingram Micro Team,
Please accept this message as notice that we intend to transition our AWS DSA to another aggregator effective April 1.
 
We appreciate the support and partnership we have with Ingram Micro and thank you for your assistance throughout our relationship.
 
We will circle back closer to April with regards to next steps and documentation needed to complete this transition.
Thanks, 
 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

Subject: [EXTERNAL]RE: Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change

Matthew Carter

From: Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com>

Hi Rick,

 

It has been awhile.  I just wanted to advise you that I put in the AWS Outbound Transfer Request for the selected accounts that we are transitioning.  Please advise if there is anything missing with the provided information on the form.

Erik

From: Marshbanks, Rick <Rick.Marshbanks@ingrammicro.com>

Subject: RE: Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change
 
+@Chandrashekar, Subramanya / @Jaroenchonwanit, Lalita
 
Hi Matt, Thank you for the note and for the partnership we have built together. While I am disappointed to hear we will not be with you for the next phase of your AWS Partner Journey, I sincerely appreciate the collaboration and trust Aptum has placed in Ingram Micro throughout our relationship. Given the April 1 transition timeline, I welcome the opportunity to schedule a brief call with the broader team to review the offboarding process to help ensure everything runs smoothly. This would also allow us to align on any country-specific considerations and required documentation so there are no disruptions.
 
I would also value the opportunity to better understand the factors that led up to this decision. If there are areas where we could have delivered more value, I want to ensure we take that feedback constructively as we continue to improve and adapt as a global business unit.  If you prefer, I am happy to connect one-on-one as well. Please let me know what works best for you and your team.
 
Regardless of the path forward, we remain committed to supporting Aptum through a seamless transition and keeping the door open for future collaboration. 
 
Respectfully, RM 
 

Rick Marshbanks 
AWS Business Development Executive
St Petersburg, FL 
(248) 622.0869
 

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 4:03 PM
To: AWS Cloud Operations <awscloudops@ingrammicro.com>; Rosario, Jonathan <Jonathan.Rosario@ingrammicro.com>
Cc: Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: [EXTERNAL]Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change 

 

Dear Ingram Micro Team,
Please accept this message as notice that we intend to transition our AWS DSA to another aggregator effective April 1.
 
We appreciate the support and partnership we have with Ingram Micro and thank you for your assistance throughout our relationship.
 
We will circle back closer to April with regards to next steps and documentation needed to complete this transition.
Thanks, 
 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain
+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Rick Marshbanks

items.body_text:
[ScopeStack Product Team]
You don't often get email from product@scopestack.io. Learn why this is important 

AI summaries, auto-save, real-time financials, and a redesigned platform experience — all live in Q2. ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­   

 

The #1 Services CPQ Platform for IT Service Providers

What's New in ScopeStack — Q2 2026
 
This quarter we shipped nine updates — including an AI executive summary generator, a fully redesigned service editor, and real-time financial tracking. Here's what's live in your account.
 
✨ AI-Generated Executive Summaries
Users with the appropriate permission can now request an AI-generated executive summary for any project — written from the services you've already scoped. One click produces a polished, client-ready summary without starting from a blank page.
 
💾 Upgraded Service Editor with Auto-Save
The project service editor has been fully rebuilt — same powerful scoping, cleaner interface, and every change saves automatically. No more manual saves, no lost work.
 
⏳ Real-Time Financials in the Project Header
Revenue, cost, and profit now update live as you scope. See the financial impact of every change in real time — no refreshing, no switching views.
 
✅ Multi-Condition Approval Rules
Business approval levels can now combine multiple conditions — for example, trigger a review when revenue exceeds $10k and the project includes custom services. More precision, fewer unnecessary approvals.
 
📋 Vendor Resource Management
Add vendor resources directly to projects with permission controls over who can edit hourly cost and price. When a resource is removed from a project, it's automatically cleaned up across all associated services.
 
✏️ Cleaner Text Editor
The text editor now uses a single shared toolbar per page and every editable region is resizable. Less clutter, more room to write.
 
🎨 Redesigned Across the Platform
The updated design system now covers Approvals, Notes, Contacts, Locations, Work Breakdown, all Pricing sections (Overall, PS, MS, Product, Payment Credits), the Clients and Accounts list, and Users & Groups.
 
All updates are live in your account. Log in to see what's changed.

See What's New 

ScopeStack is trusted by leading solution providers.
Including teams at Computacenter, SHI, ePlus, NRI, Driven Tech, and more.
 
★★★★★ 4.9/5 on G2

 

“ScopeStack provides a thoughtful and flexible CPQ framework that allows us to enhance our Professional Service development and increase the quality of service and relevance to our internal and external Customers.”
 
— Simon D, Director Services Transformation - Computacenter
 

 

ScopeStack, PO BOX 26974, Greenville, SC 29616

If you are having trouble reading this email, read the online version.

You're Invited To Our Upcoming Live Session

 

About The Live Session 

Hi Adam,
Just a reminder that our upcoming webinar, Beyond CIEM: Why Cloud Entitlement Management Is Only Half the Story, is happening soon! 
April 21 | 1 PM ET 
BeyondTrust’s David van Heerden and GigaOm’s Chris Ray will break down why CIEM alone no longer protects against today’s identity‑based threats, and what a unified, modern identity security strategy should really look like. 
You'll walk away with insights into: 
Gaps that traditional CIEM tools fail to detect 
Real cloud attack paths uncovered by Phantom Labs 
Where PAM and ITDR fit into a complete security posture 
How AI‑driven identity governance is reshaping the threat landscape

Register today!

items.subject: Deadline EOD to send the Monthly deck to DBRG
[CMH1] Crown Castle Fiber Scheduled Maintenance Notification
items.body_text:
[notification@thinkon.com]
Hello,

Thank you for being a valued ThinkOn customer. Our team of Thinkers continuously works with our partners to ensure the reliability, stability, and performance you expect from ThinkOn services.

This notification is to inform you of an upcoming planned maintenance event being performed by our network partner, Crown Castle Fiber, which may impact certain services.

Location of Work: Columbus, OH

Scheduled Start Date & Time Scheduled End Date & Time Time Zone
4/28/2026 12:00 AM 4/28/2026 6:00 AM Eastern

Expected Customer Impact: Service Affecting

Expected Impact Duration: Up to 4 hours

ThinkOn engineers will closely monitor all activities throughout the maintenance window to ensure the work is completed successfully and to minimize any potential service disruption.

Should you have any questions, concerns, or comments about this service maintenance, please reach out to us at requests@thinkon.com or by calling 1-877-787-0306.

Thank you for your continued commitment to ThinkOn—our customers are our top priority. Together, we make data thrive.

ThinkOn Operations and Customer Experience
Toll free: 1-877-787-0306
requests@thinkon.com

items.subject: Executive search insights for your leadership priorities
items.body_text:
[Amy Cutbill]
You don't often get email from cutbill@hortoninternational.com. Learn why this is important 

 
 
 

The Pressure on Executive Leadership Is Rising
 

Hi Adam
 
As 2026 progresses, leadership teams are moving from planning to execution. The strategic themes shaping this year are now playing out in real time across markets and sectors.
 
To build on our earlier perspective, we have selected three insights that explore how organisations are strengthening leadership capability and positioning for sustainable performance.
 
Strategic planning with people at the centre
Effective organisations are embedding leadership pipeline development and talent strategy directly into business planning. This integrated approach strengthens long-term resilience and ensures leadership capability keeps pace with organisational ambition.

Read more: https://hortoninternational.com/2026-strategic-planning-blueprint/
 
Developing future-ready leaders
As leadership expectations evolve, adaptability, strategic thinking, and human-centred capability are becoming defining attributes of successful executives. Building a strong leadership pipeline is now a strategic priority rather than a reactive exercise.
 
Read more: https://hortoninternational.com/leadership-pipeline-2026/
 
Leading with purpose in a digital world
Technology continues to reshape organisations, but leadership impact is increasingly defined by trust, culture, and engagement. Leaders who balance innovation with purpose and people-first decision-making set the tone for sustainable performance.
 
Read more: https://hortoninternational.com/human-side-of-2026/
 
If you'd like to discuss how these trends are playing out in your industry or region, simply reply and we'll connect you with the right specialist from our team.
 
Best regards,
The Horton International Team
 
 
Crafting Leadership, One Vision At A Time.
   

 

 

© 2025 Horton International, All rights reserved.
www.hortoninternational.com

items.subject: #cloud-platform-pm
items.body_text:
[Barbara Krzywoszanski]
Hello - *A new W-AR pricing sheet has been uploaded to the SOW folder and can be found here* - <https://disks.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/GSESalesEngagement/Shared%20Documents/Versioned%20Templates%20%26%20Assets%20-%20WIP/Base%20Templates%20-%20WIP/Templates%20with%20New%20Title%20Page/W-AR%20Pricing%20Sheet%20(April%202026).xlsx?d=w2627d5c37056433cbf6fd130c544457f&csf=1&web=1&e=2OYIdU|W-AR Pricing Sheet (April 2026).xlsx>.

The pricing sheet is meant to be used as a basis for quoting any well-architected review. Based on the recent work around W-ARs and the creation of the various templates, *we are averaging approximately 40 hours between all team members and meetings for delivery.* The work breaks down as follows:
1. Initial reporting tool installation and review
2. Workshop to review initial findings and perform further discovery on current architecture.
3. Analysis and reporting
4. Results review (Internal and Customer)
<@UPWJZLCV7> has reviewed and approved the hours and stories in the pricing sheet.

<@U015PLFUQET> <@U05UU07N5EW> - *Based on this pricing sheet the cost of a W-AR is now estimated at the following using the <https://aptum.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/KAOS/pages/4985585665/Service+Delivery+Billable+Rates+Role+Definitions|current blended rate found here>.*
• $11,000 (CAD) 
• $8,400 (USD) 
• £6,800 (GBP) 
• €7,600 (EUR)
Please let us know if we'll be using these new rates to quote to customers or if we'll be staying with the 'unofficial' $5K rate that's usually used.

If there are any questions about the pricing sheet, please reach out. Thank you - Barb
